title: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding Technique
content: Notice 5 things you can see, 4 things you can touch, 3 sounds you can hear, 2 smells you can notice, and 1 slow breath. This helps bring attention back to the present moment.

title: Box Breathing
content: Breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 4, breathe out for 4, hold for 4. Repeat 4 rounds slowly. Stop if uncomfortable and breathe naturally.

title: Study Stress Micro Plan
content: Write one small task, set a 25-minute timer, remove distractions, then take a 5-minute break. Repeat for 2 cycles before reviewing progress.

title: Deadline Overload Plan
content: List tasks as must-do, should-do, and later. Start with the smallest must-do task for 10 minutes to reduce avoidance and build momentum.

title: Sleep Hygiene Basics
content: Keep a fixed sleep time, avoid caffeine late evening, reduce bright screen exposure 30 minutes before bed, and use a short journaling brain-dump if thoughts are racing.

title: Panic First-Aid
content: Panic can feel dangerous, but it usually rises and falls. Sit down, loosen tight clothing, breathe naturally, name the current date and place, and contact help if symptoms feel medically urgent.

title: Relationship Stress Reflection
content: Separate facts, feelings, and assumptions. Write what happened, what you felt, what you need, and one respectful boundary or conversation point.

title: Loneliness Support Step
content: Send one low-pressure message to a trusted person, join a small routine activity, or write a short journal entry naming one thing you need today.

title: Motivation Tiny Goal Method
content: Choose a task so small it feels almost easy. Start for two minutes. After starting, either continue or schedule the next tiny step.

title: Career Confusion Clarity Prompt
content: Write three columns: skills you have, skills you want, and opportunities available this month. Pick one action: update resume, apply to one role, or practice one interview question.

title: Crisis Safety India
content: If there is immediate danger, call 112 in India. For mental-health support, Tele-MANAS can be reached at 14416 or 1800-891-4416. Contact a trusted person and avoid staying alone.

title: Journaling Prompt
content: Write three short lines: What am I feeling? What triggered it? What is one kind action I can take for myself today?

title: When To Seek Professional Help
content: Consider professional help if distress persists, affects sleep/work/studies, includes self-harm thoughts, panic symptoms, trauma, or feels unmanageable. AI support is not a substitute for care.
